mnemonics.title: FIFO Remember
mnemonics.content: FIFO = First In, First Out - Just imagine a FIFO queue like a checkout line where the first person in line gets served first.

mnemonics.title: LRU Remember
mnemonics.content: LRU = Least Recently Used - Think of it like your phone's 'Least Recently Used Apps' - it keeps the apps you use most.

mnemonics.title: Optimal Remember
mnemonics.content: OPT = Optimal looks forward - Like a prophet who knows the future, it knows which page won't be needed for the longest time.

mnemonics.title: Belady's Anomaly
mnemonics.content: Bigger isn't always better: More frames in FIFO can mean MORE faults - More frames, More problems (in FIFO only!).

acronyms.usage: Page replacement that evicts oldest page
Page replacement that evicts least recently accessed page
Page replacement based on access frequency
Theoretical best algorithm with minimum faults
The page not used for longest time is replaced

visualTips.visualization: Draw pages as people standing in a line. The one at the front (oldest) gets served (replaced) first. Add new people at the back.
Think of pages as books on a shelf. The book you just read goes to front. When you need space, you remove the book from the back (least recently used).
Imagine a clock face with pages around it. The clock hand scans each page - if it has been used recently (hand pointing up), give it another chance and move on. If not used, replace it.
Show FIFO with 3 frames causing 9 faults, then with 4 frames causing 10 faults - a counterintuitive result.

analogies.analogy: Like a parking lot with limited spaces - when full, you must decide which car to move out to accommodate a new one.
Like asking for a book from a library that isn't on your desk - you need to go fetch it (takes time).
Like constantly moving furniture in and out of a small room - you spend more time moving than actually working.
Like the tools on your workbench - only keep what you're actively using within reach; everything else goes in the toolbox.
